title: Degraded products returned for a Change of Mind (withdrawal) - What can you do?
bodyHtml:
If a device is returned due to a change of mind (withdrawal) in a worse condition than when you shipped it, you can offer a partial refund. To do this:

 

  • Within 2 business days after you receive the product:
    • Use the preset button to report a problem.
    • Take clear, detailed photos of the damage.
    • Be transparent about how you calculate the deductions you’ll propose to the buyer:
      • Deduct the cost of the identified damage from the refund amount.
      • If the grade difference is attributable to the buyer, apply the reductions set out in the Quality Charter (art. 3.6.3).
      • If a repair is needed due to the buyer, apply the costs listed in the table in the “Declare and manage a warranty exclusion” article, based on the specific part to repair.
      • Make sure the deductions are reasonable and proportionate to the actual loss in value.
  • If degradation is confirmed, offer the buyer either a partial refund (with the deductions above) or to keep the product with no refund.

If a full refund was issued even though you followed the process above and degradation is confirmed, contact the Seller Support Center to request compensation:

 

  • Provide the photos showing the damage.
  • Explain the nature and extent of the damage.
  • State the loss in value:
    • For a grade difference, apply the reductions set out in the Quality Charter (art. 3.6.3).
    • For repairs, apply the costs listed in the table in the “Declare and manage a warranty exclusion” article, based on the specific part to repair.

The Seller Support Center will review your request and may issue a refund under the rules above if the conditions are met.
